mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive during months of rising INDPRO (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation create a restrictive financial environment where discount rates rise faster than nominal growth, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL achieves a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms a single-factor FEDFUNDS model.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth during post-shock recovery periods drives earnings estimate revisions upward as operating leverage improves, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of post-shock recovery quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand persons signal labor market tightness that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive near-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following months with PAYEMS increases exceeding 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units indicates a contraction in residential investment that precedes broader demand weakness and negative earnings surprises, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1300.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nominal retail sales growth combined with decelerating consumer price inflation indicates healthy real consumption demand without margin-eroding cost pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or its Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 5.0 percent restricts liquidity and raises the cost of capital for corporate borrowers, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of such instances.
